items.title: Show HN: I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch
items.summary: I built a voice agent from scratch that averages ~400ms end-to-end latency (phone stop → first syllable). That’s with full STT → LLM → TTS in the loop, clean barge-ins, and no precomputed responses. What moved the needle: Voice is a turn-taking problem, not a transcription problem. VAD alone fails;

items.title: What if the real risk of AI isn’t deepfakes — but daily whispers?
items.summary: Most people don’t appreciate the profound threat that AI will soon pose to human agency . A common refrain is that “AI is just a tool,” and like any tool, its benefits and dangers depend on how people use it. This is old-school thinking. AI is transitioning from tools we use to prosthetics we wear.
